course_description:
This is a restricted entry course. To be eligible to apply to POLS3801 students must (a) be enrolled in a program in which POLS3801 can be credited, (b) completed at least 12 units of POLS courses, and (c) achieved a GPA of 5 or higher for these courses. Eligible applicants are not automatically guaranteed a place in the course. The Internship is based on a mutually agreed partnership between the student and an external organisation. Interns are expected to carry out a project on a topic/issue of direct interest to the external organisation. Established internship partners include: Queensland Parliament, Queensland Government Departments, MPs and Senator's Electoral Offices, Asia-Pacific Centre for the Responsibility to Protect (UQ) and the Centre for Policy Futures (UQ), and other national and international, government and non-government agencies. All additional costs incurred by students undertaking this course remain the responsibility of the student. Please note: Students accepted to participate in the PEATLI project over the winter break may be eligible to enrol in POLS3801 in Semester 2. Students participating in the UQ Embassy Internship Program in Summer semester may also be eligible to enrol in POLS3801 in Semester 1 or Semester 2. Further information including how to apply is available here .

An internship within the School of Political Science and International Studies is an elective 2-unit course. To be considered for the POLS3801 internship, students need to be enrolled in an undergraduate degree at the University of Queensland and have completed 2 years of full-time equivalent study.ﾠ

The internship is designedﾠto provide students with the opportunity to utilise the research and report writing skills developed throughout their undergraduate program in a relevant work context. A wide variety of organisations may offer internships, in the public, private, and community sectors.

Students who enrol in an internship are expected to produce a high quality research report based on the agreed research topic conducted for the internship. Students are placed in the most appropriate environment to ensure they will be able to further their research skills and expand on their knowledge.

Currently students are placed with a variety of organisations, depending on availabilityﾠand locational convenience. In previous years these opportunities have included placements in the offices of elected representatives in the three levels of government (Local, State and Federal). In second semester only, there is also a special program managed throughﾠthe Queensland Parliament. There are usually severalﾠopportunities to work with private sector consultancies, research institutes, and a variety of community and advocacy organisations. Very occasionally there may be opportunities in public service departments and statutory authorities.ﾠ

The School endeavours toﾠensure that placements are available in all research areas relevant to the majors taught within the School and for social science and humanities students more broadly.ﾠ

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
32 units of courses including 12 units of POLS courses
restrictions: Required GPA of 5.0 across 12 units of POLS courses

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
There is a limited formal teaching program for this course. Students enrolled in an Internship will be primarily engaged in theﾠpreparation of a research report for their Internship organisation and are likely to spend up to one day per week with their designated organisation. The research report is supervised jointly by the student's mentor in the Internship organisation and by an academic staff member of the School of Political Science and International Studies. The classes scheduled for this course will be primarily concerned with organisational issues related to the course,ﾠguidance in report writing requirements and skills, and providing support for interns in their placements. In addition, students undertaking the QPRI (Queensland Parliament) placement will have specific tasks set by the Parliamentary staff.
course_aims: POLS3801 aims to establish Internship placements that provide students with the opportunity to utilise the analytical, research and communication skills developed throughout their undergraduate program in a relevant work context.ﾠIn particular, the course seeks to:

learning_outcomes.description: Clearly define the scope and limitations of a policy research topic in conjunction with leaders in a partner organisation.
Design, develop and implement an in-depth analysis of an identified workplace issue or problem.
Co-develop policy improvements where relevant in conjunction with supervisors.
Produce a comprehensive and professional report addressing the issue or problem.
Engage in a professional, ethical and productive manner with team colleagues in the partner organisation.
Demonstrate professional behaviour appropriate to an early career professional.
Identify and articulate skills and knowledge developed through workplace professional experience.

assessment_summary.title: Downloadable form (to be signed by supervisor)

assessment_details.title: Downloadable form (to be signed by supervisor)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students must complete a two page 'project outline' (to be submitted via Turnitin ), which clearly states: 1. Agreed research question - make sure you have a well-defined question and ensure your supervisor and you are in agreement about what it is you will research. 2. Agreed method(s) - Agree with your supervisor which qualitative or quantitative method(s) you will be using to examine the above research question. This should be short but substantive. For example, 'desktop research' is inadequate. You should be more illuminating, e.g. 'Comparative case studies of legislation in Queensland, New South Wales, and Victoria. The key axes of comparison will be: aims and effectiveness, with effectiveness judged by a systematic review of the relevant literature.' PLEASE NOTE - The Project Outline & Internship Agreement (POIA) Form can be downloaded from the course's Blackboard site (learning resources section) and should be uploaded onto Turnitin before the due date once completed. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission:
Assignments for this course will be submitted electronically via Blackboard and using Turnitin .
In uploading an assignment via Turnitin you are certifying that it is your original work, that it has not been copied in whole or part from another person or source except where this is properly acknowledged, and that it has not in whole or part been previously submitted for assessment in any other course at this or any other university.
When you successfully submit your assessment to Turnitin after previewing the uploaded document (to make sure that you have chosen the correct file), you should see the “Submission Complete!” message. After this, a downloadable Digital Receipt will display on your Assignment Dashboard. It is your responsibility to download the Digital Receipt as proof of submission. Turnitin will not send this receipt to you automatically.
If you don’t see the downloadable receipt on your assignment dashboard, you should regard your submission as unsuccessful.
If the submission was not successful:
Note the error message (preferably take a screenshot). Go to your assignment page and see if it is possible to submit again. If you cannot submit again, then email your course coordinator immediately.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Unless an extension is granted, penalties for late submission apply. Students are penalised 10% of the maximum possible mark allocated for the assessment item for every calendar day that an assessment item is late.
Marks will be deducted each day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point the submitted item will not receive any marks unless an extension has been approved. Each 24-hour block is triggered from the time the submission is due.

assessment_details.title: Context Essay (1500 words)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assignment requires you to describe the organization's history, its aim(s), its organizational structure, its working procedures, and the wider context within which the organization is seeking to fulfil its mission. Special arrangements apply for the QPRI interns who may use their "Parliamentary Procedures" paper for this submission and follow the QPRI guidelines rather than the above writing guidelines. All other interns are expected to adhere to the above guidelines when writing their Organization & Context paper. Submission: This assignment must be submitted to the School of Political Science and International Studies using Turnitin (for marking purposes). Plagiarism Warning: Please ensure your assignment meets the UQ Academic Integrity standards (see POLSIS Essay Guide and/or UQ's website on Academic Integrity). Assignments containing one or more sections paraphrased or copied verbatim from other sources without adequate referencing will be reported to the POLSIS Integrity Officer, who will subsequently subject the assignment to closer inspection and reach a decision as to whether penalties for academic misconduct should be applied. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Presentation (5-minute snapshot)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Interns are expected to present a 'short and snappy' 5-minute overview of their internship research/report and then answer a few questions from fellow students and the coordinator. Interns are expected to prepare and use a PowerPoint (maximum 6 slides), and to address the following five points; 1 - The aim(s) of the research 2 - The central research question 3 - The method(s) being deployed 4 - The main finding(s) 5 - Ensuing implications and/or policy recommendations This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
If you are unable to present on the due date, please submit an extension request on my.UQ. If your extension is approved, the revised due date/time for a presentation via Zoom will be arranged with the course coordinator.

assessment_details.title: Research Report (4000 words)
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Interns are to write a 4000 word internship report. The report should clearly articulate (1) the broad topic being investigated, (2) a specific central question, (3) the method used to examine this question, (4) the main research findings, and (5) the implications of these findings for current policy practice and/or stakeholder engagement. A good report will typically discuss each of these five aspects in this particular order, so you may want to consider structuring your research report in this way. For those students participating in the Queensland Parliament Research stream, the requirements of the Queensland Parliament program prevail over the arrangements suggested for other students. The completed report must be submitted (a) to the School of Political Science and International Studies using Turnitin (for marking purposes), and to the internship organisation, in either electronic OR hardcopy format, depending on the internship organization's preference. Plagiarism Warning: Please ensure your assignment meets the UQ Academic Integrity standards (see POLSIS Essay Guide and/or UQ's website on Academic Integrity). Assignments containing one or more sections paraphrased or copied verbatim from other sources without adequate referencing will be reported to the POLSIS Integrity Officer, who will subsequently subject the assignment to closer inspection and reach a decision as to whether penalties for academic misconduct should be applied. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_resources.library_resources: Library resources are available on the UQ Library website .

learning_activities.topic: Seminar 1 INDUCTION SEMINAR - 31 July 2025 Learning outcomes: L01

Getting to know the organization - Familiarize yourself with the organization's overall aim(s) - Gain a solid understanding of the aim(s) of your project - Ensure you are in agreement with your supervisor Learning outcomes: L01, L04, L05, L06, L07

Reaching agreement about the research project - Articulate the research question your research will examine - Consider potential research methods (in addition to Literature Reviewing) - Ensure you are in agreement with your supervisor - Apply for Ethical Clearance if research involves human participants Learning outcomes: L01, L04
Seminar 2 RESEARCH SCOPING SEMINAR - 21 August 2025 Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05, L06, L07

Reviewing relevant social science literature - Review the existing literature examining your research topic - Identify relevant existing knowledge and possible knowledge gaps - Decide whether literature reviewing will suffice to meet organization's needs Learning outcomes: L05

Independent Research - Review the existing literature examining your research topic - Identify relevant existing knowledge and possible knowledge gaps - Decide whether literature reviewing will suffice to meet organization's needs Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Independent research Finalise 'Literature Review' section for the final research report - Deploy additional research method (if necessary) - Data collection (statistics, policy documents, archives, survey/interviews*) * NOTE: Ethical Clearance required if research involves human participants Learning outcomes: L02, L06, L07
Seminar 3 RESEARCH & RESEARCH REPORTING SEMINAR - 18 September 2025 Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05
Independent research Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05

Independent research - Data collection (statistics, policy documents, archives, survey/interviews) -- Write and/or revise draft research report, incorporate feedback Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04, L05
Seminar 4 16 October 2025 Oral Presentations on research project (5 minute overview with powerpoint slides) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06, L07
Independent research and Report writing revisions of draft report, consult with supervisors and agree on presentation and referencing style Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
Independent research and Report Submission - Write/revise final research report - Submit Final (4000 word) Research Report (see Section 6. Assessment) Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library .
